title: China to end vehicle tax exemption for PHEVs and REEVs in 2027
author: Benchmark Team
text:
Starting 1 January 2027, China will remove the annual “Vehicle and Vessel” tax exemption that has applied since 2012 to: plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), including range-extended EVs (REEVs); commercial battery electric vehicles (BEV); and fuel-cell commercial vehicles. 

China's Ministry of Finance, the State Taxation Administration, and the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT) jointly issued the policy on 3 July 2026. 

BEV and fuel-cell passenger cars remain fully exempt from the tax as those taxes are based on engine displacement and these vehicles have none, therefore, they fall outside its scope entirely.  Continue reading Create a free account to access this content and read up to 3 subscriber articles each month Sign Up Already a user?
